verses.2: The godly man has perished out of the earth,
verses.3: Their hands are on that which is evil to do it diligently.
verses.4: The best of them is like a brier.
verses.6: For the son dishonors the father,
verses.7: But as for me, I will look to the LORD.
verses.8: Don’t rejoice against me, my enemy.
verses.9: I will bear the indignation of the LORD,
verses.10: Then my enemy will see it,
verses.11: A day to build your walls!
verses.12: In that day they will come to you from Assyria and the cities of Egypt,
verses.13: Yet the land will be desolate because of those who dwell therein,
verses.14: Shepherd your people with your staff,
verses.15: “As in the days of your coming out of the land of Egypt,
verses.16: The nations will see and be ashamed of all their might.
verses.17: They will lick the dust like a serpent.
verses.18: Who is a God like you, who pardons iniquity,
verses.19: He will again have compassion on us.
verses.20: You will give truth to Jacob,
